channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Attorney General Pam Bondi rails against New York leaders as she announces immigration lawsuit
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's newly installed attorney general, Pam Bondi, went after New York leaders Wednesday over the state's immigration policies, announcing a lawsuit in the latest effort by the Republican administration to carry out the president's hardline immigration campaign p
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans unveil blueprint to extend $4.5 trillion in tax cuts and lift the debt ceiling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans released a budget plan Wednesday that sets the stage for advancing many of President Donald Trump's top domestic priorities, providing for up to $4.5 trillion in tax cuts and a $4 trillion increase in the debt limit so the U.S. can continue financing its bills.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Skyrocketing fees: St. Pete condo owners say they never asked for bailout, only relief
item.description: Condo owners are waiting for answers on whether lawmakers will do anything to ease concerns over rising fees.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: ACLU sues for access to migrants flown to Guantanamo this month
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Civil rights attorneys sued the Trump administration on Wednesday to gain access to detained migrants who they say have been flown to Guantanamo Bay and held there without being able to consult lawyers or speak to relatives. The federal lawsuit, filed in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans advance anti-abortion legislation during control of Minnesota House
item.description: Republicans have control of the Minnesota House at least through a special election in March, giving them a chance to advance abortion legislation that would have a tougher path in a tied chamber.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Chicago mayor's office gives inside look at 'gift room' after watchdog report flagged ethical concerns
item.description: Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson released a 20-second video showing the inside of the city's "gift room" in an attempt at transparency. He was accused of improperly accepting gifts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Attorney general: US sues New York officials over immigration enforcement
item.description: Bondi said the lawsuit takes aim at a "green light law" that allows people who are living in the country illegally to obtain driver's licenses in New York.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Pro-Trump California sheriff expected to run for governor
item.description: Speculation is mounting that Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco, an outspoken supporter of President Trump, will throw his hat into the race for California Governor next year.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bad landlords target of bill that provides tools to help Las Vegas tenants
item.description: Nevada cities and counties don't have a lot of options when a landlord ignores poor conditions that are putting tenants in danger. Lawmakers could use AB211 to change that.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge won't halt Trump, Musk federal worker buyout program
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge George O’Toole found that the unions who had sued over the directive did not have standing to do so.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Judge restores Trump administration's buyout offer to federal workers
item.description: A federal judge in Massachusetts restored President Donald Trump's deferred resignation program in a decision Wednesday night after the OPM offered buyouts to 2 million workers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House GOP's budget impasse thaws just as winter storm sacks Capitol
item.description: As snow falls on D.C., House Republicans push forward on President Donald Trump's agenda with multiple bills and visits to the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No longer taken advantage of: Trump signs order prioritizing 'unified' US foreign policy front
item.description: A new executive order emphasizes the State Department's responsibility to uphold President Donald Trump's foreign policy priorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Certain criminal suspects would be denied bail under changes OK'd by Texas Senate panel
item.description: The package of bills would tighten the state's bail laws to keep more people in jail — including undocumented immigrants — while their criminal cases are pending.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Pooja Salhotra And Jasper Scherer, KPRC 2

item.title: Feds to probe Minnesota State High School League over transgender athlete policy
item.description: The Trump administration on Wednesday said it will investigate the league’s intention to follow state law and allow students in the state to participate in sports consistent with their gender identity, despite the president’s recent executive order
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Tulsi Gabbard Confirmed
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republicans unveil blueprint to advance Trump's agenda
item.description: House Republicans on Wednesday released a blueprint they intend to use to advance President Trump’s legislative agenda, breaking through an impasse that had stymied the conference for days.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: White House acknowledges disagreement with Jordan over relocating Palestinians
item.description: The White House on Wednesday acknowledged disagreement between President Trump and King Abdullah II of Jordon over Trump's pitch to relocate Palestinians from the Gaza Strip while the U.S. takes over the territory.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: USAID staffers describe colleagues abandoned in violence in Congo as DOGE ends assistance programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A lieutenant of Trump ally Elon Musk and other outsiders are overseeing the immediate termination of hundreds of American aid and foreign assistance programs abroad this week, without required documentation or justification, according to newly filed affidavits from staffers and acc
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell only Republican to vote against Gabbard
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) on Wednesday voted against confirming former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) to serve as the nation’s top intelligence official, sending a strong message to fellow GOP senators, some of whom privately doubted her qualifications to hold the job.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Gabbard as Trump's director of national intelligence after Republicans fall in line
item.description: The vote Wednesday came after Republicans who had initially questioned her experience and judgment fell in line behind her nomination.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms Gabbard to serve as nation’s top intelligence chief
item.description: The Senate voted largely along party lines Wednesday to confirm Tulsi Gabbard to serve as the director of national intelligence, the nation’s top intelligence official, despite strong objections from Democrats and initial misgivings from Republicans who questioned her experience and judgment.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal health agencies restore webpages and datasets following judge's order
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal health agencies have restored several webpages and datasets, following a judge's order to bring back public access to information that had been removed to comply with a presidential executive order.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE and KASTURI PANANJADY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schumer: Democrats will use spending bills to curb Musk
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.) says Democrats will push for language in the spending bills that need to pass by March 14 that would unwind Elon Musk’s efforts to dismantle government agencies such as the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump official targeting Jan. 6 investigators worked on those cases himself
item.description: The disconnect between Bove's aggressive stance at the time to hold rioters accountable — and his current hostility towards the Jan. 6 probe — has troubled some former colleagues.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk's DOGE takes aim at agency that had plans of regulating X
item.description: Musk and other Silicon Valley executives have long railed against the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for its work overseeing the tech industry. Now, Musk hopes to eliminate the agency.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump made millions on meme coin, over 800K people lost money: Report
item.description: The price of $Trump has dropped to around $17, resulting in cumulative losses of $2 billion for several investors.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Egg prices unite Nevada lawmakers as AB171 makes its way toward approval
item.description: Nevada lawmakers continued Tuesday to fast-track legislation that could bring down the price of eggs, pushing Assembly Bill 171 (AB171) closer to law.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gov. Hochul reacts to pending dismissal of corruption case against Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The pending dismissal of the corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams has partners in running the city and political opponents raising concerns about whether Adams can run the city. Gov. Kathy Hochul even said she does not know if the mayor is “compromised.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Jan. 6 video evidence has 'disappeared' from public access, media coalition says
item.description: A coalition of news organizations said in a court filing that video exhibits from a Jan. 6 riot case had "disappeared" from a government platform that provided access to evidence used in court.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: Musk defends DOGE alongside Trump in striking Oval Office moment
item.description: “The people voted for major government reform and that’s what the people are going to get,” Musk said. “That’s what democracy is all about.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada lawmaker moves to put Indigenous Peoples' Day on Columbus Day
item.description: Nevada law says Indigenous Peoples Day falls on Aug. 9, but a lawmaker is trying to change that with AB144, which would set the observation on the second Monday in October — the same day as Colombus Day.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pentagon changes Fort Liberty's name back to Fort Bragg
item.description: The Pentagon will change the name of the North Carolina military base Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, fulfilling a campaign pledge by President Trump while honoring a different eponym.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope criticizes Trump administration's immigration policies
item.description: Pope Francis criticized President Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans in a letter urging compassion and the preservation of dignity for migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Nearly half of NY voters don’t want Mayor Adams’ charges dropped: poll
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Nearly half of New Yorkers oppose Mayor Eric Adams’ charges being dropped, according to a recent PIX11 poll.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams to speak after DOJ orders prosecutors to drop charges
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams spoke on Tuesday after the Department of Justice ordered federal prosecutors to drop the bribery and corruption charges against him.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration targets Education Department research arm in latest cuts
item.description: The Institute of Education Sciences (IES) is responsible for gathering data on a wide range of topics, including research-backed teaching practices and the state of U.S. student achievement.
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta and Cory Turner, MPR News
